commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: These things, he means, are terrible; and so terrible that they could not even bear to hear them, that not even a beast dared to go up. (But things that come hereafter are not such. For what is Sina to Heaven? And what the fire which might be touched to God who cannot be touched? For God is a consuming fire. Hebrews 5:29) For it is said, Let not God speak, but let Moses speak unto us. And so fearful was that which was commanded, Though even a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned; Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake. Exodus 20:19 What wonder as respects the people? He himself who entered into the darkness where God was, says, I exceedingly fear and quake. Exodus 20:21

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
